title: Word Ladder II
problem_statement: A transformation sequence from word beginWord to word endWord using a dictionary wordList is a sequence of words beginWord -> s1 -> s2 -> ... -> sk such that: Every adjacent pair of words differs by a single letter. Every si for 1 <= i <= k is in wordList. All words in the sequence are of the same length. Note that beginWord does not need to be in wordList. sk == endWord. Given two words, beginWord and endWord, and a dictionary wordList, return all the shortest transformation sequences from beginWord to endWord, or an empty list if there is no such sequence. The sequences should be returned in any order.

examples.explanation: There are 2 shortest transformation sequences, each of length 5.
The endWord "cog" is not in wordList, therefore there is no valid transformation sequence.

constraints: beginWord, endWord, and wordList[i] consist of lowercase English letters.
All the words in wordList are unique.
intuition: Use BFS to find shortest distance and build parent map, then DFS to generate all paths.

approaches.name: BFS + DFS Backtracking
approaches.description: BFS to build adjacency for shortest paths, then DFS from end to start to collect all paths.
approaches.why_not: BFS alone gives distance, DFS builds paths.
interview_commentary: BFS builds children map for shortest paths.
DFS backtracks from begin to end using children.

interview_qa.a: Ensures only shortest paths are considered.
